questions.question:
Which symbol makes the following statement true? 
$$0.3 \quad \underline{\quad} \quad 0.29$$
questions.explanation: If we pad $0.3$ with a zero, it becomes $0.30$. Comparing tenths: $3 > 2$, so $0.30 > 0.29$.

questions.question: Which of the following decimals is the largest?
questions.explanation: Padded with zeros: $0.800$, $0.790$, $0.089$, $0.810$. Comparing left-to-right, $0.810$ has the highest combination of tenths and hundredths.

questions.question: Arrange $0.15$, $0.2$, and $0.095$ in ascending order (smallest to largest).
questions.explanation: Converting all to three decimal places: $0.095$, $0.150$, $0.200$. Since $95 < 150 < 200$ hundredths/thousandths, the correct order is $0.095, 0.15, 0.2$.

questions.question: Which statement is true regarding the decimals $0.6$ and $0.600$?
options: $0.600$ is larger because it has more digits.
$0.6$ is larger because it is closer to the decimal point.
They are equal because adding trailing zeros does not change a decimal's value.
questions.explanation: Adding zeros at the end of a decimal number does not add any value (it represents 0 hundredths and 0 thousandths), so $0.6 = 0.600$.

questions.question: Which relationship is correct?
questions.explanation: Padding $2.54$ yields $2.540$. Comparing thousandths: $0$ is less than $1$ ($0 < 1$). Thus, $2.540 < 2.541$.
